topics.label: Running AWK
topics.description: Use awk on the command line or in scripts. AWK processes text line by line.

topics.label: Pattern-Action Rules
topics.description: pattern { action } — process lines matching patterns. BEGIN/END for setup/teardown.

topics.label: Fields ($1, $2, NF)
topics.description: Auto-split lines into fields. $0 is the whole line, $1 is the first field.

topics.label: Built-in Variables
topics.description: NR (line number), NF (field count), FS (field separator), OFS, RS.

topics.label: Control Flow & Functions
topics.description: if/else, for, while, and user-defined functions.

topics.label: String Functions
topics.description: sub, gsub, match, split, sprintf, substr, length.

topics.label: Associative Arrays
topics.description: array[key] = value. AWK arrays are hash maps — powerful for counting and grouping.

topics.label: Practical One-Liners
topics.description: Sum columns, count patterns, transform CSV, generate reports. AWK excels at data extraction.
